programmes.show_description: Carry On, Hawkeye: A flu epidemic leaves Trapper, Frank and Henry out of action. In this Korea-wide epidemic they can't find anyone to come and help with casualties besides Hawkeye.
The Incubator: Hawkeye and Trapper desperately try to acquire an incubator for the camp. They meet Major Morris, a medical officer with three incubators who refuses to let them have one.
Paper Chase: Henry is concerned about Matthew's lack of effort towards his upcoming exams.
Bridge of Sighs: Henry's attention turns to the prospect of love and romance when his ex-girlfriend, who has been living in America for the last thirty years, returns to the UK for a visit.
Front Runner: Henry suggests that Matthew should get a job to supplement his college grant. But when Matthew gets a job at his father's firm, will this ultimately help or hinder Henry?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
With Love, The Claus: It's Christmas and Jonathan and Mark's latest assignment is to stop a divorced couple from bickering with each other for their son's sake.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
A Mother's Love: Four recently orphaned brothers are informed by the social worker assigned to their case that the younger children will be placed in different foster homes.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

In with the 'In' Crowd: An undercover cop investigating a drug ring at a local high school faces suspension because of a mistake Mark has made.
With Love, The Claus: It's Christmas and Jonathan and Mark's latest assignment is to stop a divorced couple from bickering with each other for their son's sake.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Last Summer: Jason Carter starts working for Ruthy Leland. During their time together he learns the power of friendship as well as the painful lessons that sometimes come with it.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: For The Love Of Blanche: Mr Edwards is left in care of a baby orangutan named Blanche, whom Mrs Olesen wants destroyed after it attacked Nancy at school.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: May I Have This Dance?: When Willie announces his plans to marry sweetheart Rachel Brown and manage the hotel-restaurant bearing his sister's name, Mrs Oleson tries to interfere.
The Collision: A former girlfriend returns to Walton Mountain and challenges John-Boy to re-examine his life's goals.
The Burn Out: Part 1: A flash fire demolishes the second floor of the Waltons' house and begins a series of events that threatens to destroy the family.
Amongst Friends: Peter has to make some important decisions about where his future lies. Despite it being a difficult time, Quigley carries on with his business ventures.
His Brother's Keeper: A former German soldier arrives in Ormston, desperately wanting to speak to Wilf Bradshaw. Arthur proves to be a troublesome patient after breaking his ankle.
No Time for Heroes: The son of the local headmaster is diagnosed with diphtheria, but prompt action by Dr Napier saves his life. Brenda makes a surprise return to help.
Rumpole and the Family Pride: Rumpole's invitation to Sackbut Castle soon becomes a work assignment when he is asked to investigate a suspicious drowning.
The General Flipped at Dawn: Major General Steele, the new area commander, is about to arrive at the 4077th. He appears to be more eccentric than the usual army brass.
Rainbow Bridge: Hawkeye and Trapper are sent into enemy territory with no artillery when captured G.I.s need medical attention. Frank's nervous disposition threatens to ruin the arrangement.
Officer of the Day: Lt Col Henry is away and Frank is in charge. Col Flagg of Intelligence has a wounded North Korean who needs to get to Seoul for interrogation and then execution as a spy.
Iron Guts Kelly: Famed General 'Iron Guts' Kelly arrives for an inspection and dies in action, but not the kind of death that would look good on a war record.
O.R.: The wounded arrive by helicopter, ambulance and jeep, leaving the 4077th overwhelmed by the casualties. Everyone is called into action while Hawkeye, Trapper and Henry work quickly.
Springtime: Several wild events happen during a spring day in Korea. Klinger receives a letter from his girl Laverne who agrees to marry him. Next, Radar falls for a nurse.
Check-up: All personnel are ordered to have a medical check-up. Trapper refuses an examination and finally confesses he's got an ulcer which could provide grounds for a medical discharge.
To Catch A Rat: Steed, Purdey, and Gambit are called into action when a former agent reappears after having vanished for almost two decades.
Whoever Shot Poor George Oblique Stroke XR40?: Having unearthed a plot to sabotage the country's most powerful computer, The Avengers call upon its creator for help to solve the case.
The General Flipped at Dawn: Major General Steele, the new area commander, is about to arrive at the 4077th. He appears to be more eccentric than the usual army brass.
Rainbow Bridge: Hawkeye and Trapper are sent into enemy territory with no artillery when captured G.I.s need medical attention. Frank's nervous disposition threatens to ruin the arrangement.
Officer of the Day: Lt Col Henry is away and Frank is in charge. Col Flagg of Intelligence has a wounded North Korean who needs to get to Seoul for interrogation and then execution as a spy.
Return to Clagthorpe: Matthew is disappointed to learn of his father's plans for their holiday. Instead of going somewhere exotic, Henry has decided to take them to a familiar guesthouse.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Country Doctor: A small town doctor is in bad health but he can't afford to retire and there is no one else to take his place.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Time in a Bottle: An ambitious district attorney clashes with an alcoholic lawyer. However, he agrees to defend a fellow denizen charged with stealing a cheap bottle of wine.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Hello And Goodbye: Mr Edwards faces a moral dilemma when Philip Rogers, the biological father of his recently adopted son Matthew, shows up to reclaim the boy.
...To Yesterday: Albert decides to stay in Walnut Grove after he receives some heartbreaking news. Meanwhile, the schoolchildren prepare for the traditional climbing of the mountain. (1983)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
...To Yesterday: Albert decides to stay in Walnut Grove after he receives some heartbreaking news. Meanwhile, the schoolchildren prepare for the traditional climbing of the mountain. (1983)
The Burn Out: Part 2: The family is challenged by the fire but becomes stronger from the ordeal.
The First Edition: John-Boy launches his first major newspaper venture as editor and publisher of the Blue Ridge Chronicle. Will his determination to search for the truth divide his family?
All Bar One: As Fitzgerald's bar is up for auction, a number of new faces arrive in Ballykissangel, some more welcome than others.
Old Flames: Love is in the air as Deborah's mother arrives in Ormston, but will the build-up to the big day be straightforward? Elsewhere, Tom is reunited with his first love.
All Jock Tamson's Bairns: The Brigadier, a wartime hero, dies in hospital of a heart attack. Many of the villagers mistakenly believe that his death was caused by typhus.
Rumpole on Trial: Rumpole is in an irritable mood after visiting the dentist. But will his temper get the better of him in court during a manslaughter case? Peter Sallis stars.
Life with Father: Henry's wife writes to him that, given the circumstances of war, she'd understand if he found another woman. He reads between the lines that she is having an affair.
Alcoholics Unanimous: As commanding officer, Frank dismantles the Swamp's still and declares Prohibition at the 4077th. Hawkeye and Trapper get irritable and wonder if they're alcoholics.
There is Nothing Like a Nurse: The threat of an enemy parachute drop forces the 4077th to evacuate the nurses to another unit. The camp realises just how much the nurses mean to the unit.
Jacko's interest in football is revived-much to Eric and Jean's amusement.
Jacko helps Elmo make a promotional video, which promotes trouble.
Jacko loses his home and his job, and things look so desperate that for a moment an offer from Elmo of free board and lodging above the wine bar looks tempting. But only for a moment.
The Tale Of The Big Why: A prisoner finds himself in danger on his release from prison. But can the team keep him and his secret safe?
